'''Jamie Ellis''' (born 4 October 1989) is a former English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|sh|so}} or {{rlp|hk}} for [[Oldham RLFC]] in [[RFL League 1]].

He has previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] during 2017's [[Super League XXII]], to which he returned on a 1-year deal for 2020's [[Super League XXV]].

==Background==
Ellis was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside]], England.

==Early career==
Ellis was as a youngster with the amateur team Chorley Panthers.

==Playing career==
===St Helens===
Ellis made his professional début in a 10–20 defeat by [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 2009's [[Super League XIV]].<ref name="St Helens 10-20 Salford">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/106 |title=St Helens 10-20 Salford |publisher=saintsrlfc.com |date=2009-07-06 |access-date=2009-07-06 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090707021009/http://www.saintsrlfc.com/results/106 |archive-date=2009-07-07 }}</ref> He since featured against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in 2009's [[Super League XIV Results#Round 22|Super League XIV Round 22]] game, where he came off the bench to be utilised as a {{rlp|hk}}.<ref name="St Helens 44-24 Harlequins">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/109 |title=St Helens 44-24 Harlequins |publisher=saintsrlfc.com |date=2009-07-25 |access-date=2009-07-27 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101129091636/http://saintsrlfc.com/results/109 |archive-date=2010-11-29 }}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
At the end of 2010's [[Super League XV]], Ellis left St. Helens, after falling out of favour with new coach [[Royce Simmons]], and a run of bad injuries saw him leave the Super League side. He joined [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] outfit Leigh Centurions on a one-year contract.<ref name="Ellis leaves Saints">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=20602 |title=Ellis leaves Saints |publisher=superleague.co.uk |date=2010-11-25 |access-date=2010-11-26 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101128192454/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=20602 |archive-date=2010-11-28 }}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
In May 2011 it was confirmed that Ellis would be moving to [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]] for the start of the 2012 campaign.<ref name="Centurions' Jamie Ellis agrees terms to move to KC">{{cite web|url=http://www.thisishullandeastriding.co.uk/sport/Undefined-Headline/article-3602174-detail/article.html|title=Centurions' Jamie Ellis agrees terms on move to KC|publisher=thisishullandeastriding.co.uk|date=2011-05-27|access-date=2011-05-28}}{{Dead link|date=August 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
In June 2012, Ellis signed a contract with the Castleford Tigers for the 2013 season where he then left at the end of 2014.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18280213|title= Castleford Tigers|access-date=2012-06-26}}</ref>

Ellis played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
In October 2014, it was announced that he would join the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a two-year deal starting in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|title=Jamie Ellis: Huddersfield Giants sign Castleford Tigers half-back|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29441200|website=BBC Sport|access-date=17 January 2015|date=1 October 2014}}</ref>
[[File:Jamie Ellis.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Ellis playing for the Huddersfield Giants in 2015]]

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In 2016, Ellis joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a season-long loan deal, citing Head Coach [[Tim Sheens|Tim Sheens']] intentions of getting Hull Kingston Rovers back into the Super League.<ref>{{cite web |title=Jamie Ellis: Hull KR sign Huddersfield Giants half-back as five leave |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37942982 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=1 July 2025 |date=10 November 2016}}</ref> On 6 December 2019 Ellis returned to Hull Kingston Rovers on a one-year contract for 2020.

===Castleford Tigers (re-join)===
In 2017 Ellis signed again with Castleford on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5697|title=Ellis returns to the Tigers|publisher=Castleford Tigers|date=18 October 2017|access-date=8 December 2017}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (re-join)===
Ellis re-signed for Leigh for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/jamie-ellis-to-depart-club-after-2020/|title=JAMIE ELLIS TO DEPART CLUB AFTER 2020|website=www.hullkr.co.uk|access-date=13 Dec 2020}}</ref> He also spent a short two-week loan spell at [[York City Knights]] in Jun 2021.

===York City Knights===
On 30 September 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Sep 2021|accessdate=31 Dec 2021|title=Jamie Ellis heading to Championship next season|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamie-ellis-heading-to-championship/}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 14 July 2023 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Jul 2023|accessdate=14 Jul 2023|title=Jamie Ellis: Former Super League star amongst two signings for promotion-chasing Oldham|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamie-ellis-former-super-league-star-amongst-two-signings-for-promotion-chasing-oldham}}</ref>
